symptoms.symptom_name: Severe worst-of-kind headache with photophobia/phonophobia and flushing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe headache with flushing and sensory hypersensitivity fits a mast cell neuro-inflammatory flare.
A severe worst-of-kind headache mandates SAH consideration.
Severe headache with photophobia mandates meningitis consideration.
A gradual build-up is less typical of SAH than an instantaneous thunderclap, but does not exclude it.
A supple neck and afebrile status lower meningitis probability, though early meningitis can lack these and needs in-person assessment.
Cognitive fog accompanies mast cell flares, but new confusion with a severe headache demands intracranial exclusion.
Confusion is a concerning non-specific feature that cannot alone distinguish benign fog from encephalitic processes.

symptoms.symptom_name: No projectile vomiting, no focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of vomiting/focal deficit is reassuring but not exclusionary.
Poor analgesic response is non-specific; it does not localise the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves/motor, supple neck, negative meningeal signs, no papilloedema (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neuro exam with negative meningeal signs supports a benign flare in person.
Negative meningeal signs argue against meningitis, though not definitively.
Visible flushing supports a mast cell mediator process accompanying the headache.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe headache with flushing, photophobia and cognitive fog most likely reflects a mast cell neuro-inflammatory flare, but new severe headache with confusion is a headache red flag; meningitis and SAH cannot be excluded remotely (no meningeal signs/fundoscopy over chat), so urgent in-person assessment is required, with 000 for any thunderclap onset, neck stiffness, fever, focal deficit or worsening consciousness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person neurological assessment (meningeal signs, fundoscopy, GCS)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude meningitis/SAH; cannot be done via chat, and confusion is present.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT brain ± lumbar puncture if intracranial pathology suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude SAH/meningitis if the in-person assessment raises concern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum tryptase (if seen acutely) and mast cell work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To support the mast cell diagnosis once serious causes are excluded.
